title: Raccoon Removal Great Neck, NY | Professional Wildlife Control $900-$4,000
metaDescription: Expert raccoon removal services in Great Neck, Long Island. Licensed wildlife control specialists. Same-day response. Humane trapping & exclusion. Call for free estimate!
intro: Great Neck residents facing raccoon intrusions need prompt, professional wildlife removal services to protect their homes and families. Our experienced team specializes in humane raccoon trapping, exclusion, and damage repair throughout Great Neck, Great Neck Plaza, Kings Point, and surrounding North Shore communities. We understand the unique challenges posed by raccoons in Long Island's suburban environment and provide comprehensive solutions tailored to your property.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Great Neck homeowners for years with licensed, insured wildlife specialists who prioritize humane removal methods and permanent solutions. Our team provides same-day emergency response, thorough attic inspections, complete exclusion work, and sanitization services to ensure raccoons don't return to your Great Neck property.
localContext: Great Neck's tree-lined streets, historic estates, and waterfront properties near Kings Point and Saddle Rock provide ideal habitats for raccoons seeking shelter in attics, chimneys, and crawl spaces. The area's mix of older Tudor and Colonial homes along Middle Neck Road and newer construction near Great Neck Plaza often feature architectural elements that allow raccoon entry if not properly secured.

faqs.question: How much does raccoon removal cost in Great Neck?
faqs.answer: Raccoon removal in Great Neck typically costs between $900 and $4,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, number of raccoons, entry points requiring exclusion, and extent of attic damage. A basic single raccoon removal with one entry point may cost around $900-$1,500, while extensive infestations with multiple animals, babies, significant damage repair, and full-home exclusion can reach $3,000-$4,000. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before any work begins.

faqs.question: Are raccoons common in Great Neck, Long Island?
faqs.answer: Yes, raccoons are very common throughout Great Neck and the North Shore of Long Island. The area's mature trees, proximity to water, abundant food sources, and mix of residential properties provide ideal conditions for raccoons. They're particularly active in spring when mothers seek attics and chimneys for nesting, and in fall when they search for warm winter denning sites. Great Neck's older homes often have vulnerable entry points that raccoons exploit.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove raccoons from my Great Neck home?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency response for Great Neck raccoon removal services. Initial trapping setup typically occurs within 24 hours of your call. Complete removal timeframes vary: a single raccoon may be trapped within 2-5 days, while a mother with babies requires special handling and may take 1-2 weeks to safely remove all animals. Once raccoons are removed, we immediately begin exclusion work to seal entry points and prevent re-entry.

faqs.question: What damage can raccoons cause to Great Neck homes?
faqs.answer: Raccoons can cause extensive damage to Great Neck properties including torn roof shingles, damaged soffits and fascia, destroyed attic insulation, chewed electrical wiring (fire hazard), contamination from feces and urine, and structural damage to chimneys and vents. They often tear open entry holes to enlarge access points and can destroy HVAC ductwork. Their waste carries health risks including raccoon roundworm. Our service includes damage assessment, repairs, insulation replacement, and complete attic sanitization to restore your home.
